title: phyla nodiflora var. sericea
intro: Phyla nodiflora var. sericea, also known as Mexican tea or lippia, is a plant species of the Phyla genus, characterized by its hairy leaves and fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Phyla nodiflora var. sericea include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various ailments. Additionally, the plant's essential oils have been used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
